title: Market analysis brief: starter discovery trace demo
category_structure: Generic work tools centralize activity but do not guarantee a readable discovery story.
PM tools improve prioritization but still separate some upstream and downstream context.
category_summary: Teams increasingly want fewer disconnected tools and clearer accountability for why work progressed.
trend_summary: AI speeds up artifact creation, which makes clear traceability and gate discipline more important.
market_dynamics: Execution tools centralize activity but often do not explain discovery decisions.
PM tools improve prioritization but may still separate discovery context from downstream planning.
power_centers: PM operators and product ops leads often shape process adoption for starter systems.
adoption_barriers: Teams may assume a generic template is sufficient until the need for traceability becomes concrete.
switching_costs: Most teams already have ad hoc docs and process habits, even if they are weak.
market_role_implications: The starter should act like a niche operating model, not a generic template pack.
